question_structure.context: As shown in the figure, above the dashed line at a height of $h=0.8\mathrm{m}$ from the horizontal ground, there is a uniform magnetic field directed horizontally into the plane of the paper. The square wire frame abcd has a side length of $l=0.2\mathrm{m}$, a mass of $m=0.1\mathrm{kg}$, and a resistance of $R{=}0.08\Omega$. The frame is initially thrown vertically upward with a certain initial velocity. When the $ab$ side of the frame enters the magnetic field with a velocity of $v_{0}=2\mathrm{m/s}$, a constant upward force $F=2\mathbf{N}$ is applied to the frame, causing it to move at a constant velocity. After the entire frame enters the magnetic field, the external force $F$ is immediately removed. The frame continues to rise in the magnetic field for some time before it begins to fall and eventually lands on the ground. The acceleration due to gravity is taken as $g=10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.
question_structure.sub_question_1: The magnitude of the magnetic induction intensity of a uniform magnetic field
question_structure.sub_question_2: The time it takes for the wire frame to enter the magnetic field from the start until it reaches the highest point.
question_structure.sub_question_3: The magnitude of the velocity when the wireframe lands.
question_structure.sub_question_4: The Joule heat generated by the wireframe during the entire process.

sub_question_1.step_1: When the $ab$ side of the wire frame just enters the magnetic field, the magnitude of the induced current is $I=\frac{E}{R}=\frac{B lv_{0}}{R}$.
sub_question_1.step_2: The frame moves in uniform linear motion, with $F = m g + B I l$.
sub_question_1.step_3: By simultaneously solving step_1 and step_2, we obtain $B=1\mathrm{T}$.

sub_question_2.step_4: Let the time for the wire frame to enter the magnetic field and move at a constant linear velocity be $t_{1}$. We have $t_{1}=\frac{l}{v_{0}}=\frac{0.2}{2}\mathrm{s}=0.1\mathrm{s}$.
sub_question_2.step_5: After the wire frame completely enters the magnetic field, it undergoes a vertical upward projectile motion to reach the highest point, and the time taken is $t_{2}=\frac{v_{0}}{g}=\frac{2}{10}\mathrm{s}=0.2\mathrm{s}$.
sub_question_2.step_6: By combining step_4 and step_5, the time taken for the wire frame from entering the magnetic field to reaching the highest point is $t=t_{1}+t_{2}=0.3\mathrm{s}$.

sub_question_3.step_7: When the wire frame returns from the highest point to the boundary of the magnetic field, the magnitude of its velocity remains unchanged, and the magnitude of the Ampere force acting on the wire frame also remains unchanged. The direction of the force is vertically upward, and $m g = B I l$.
sub_question_3.step_8: The wire frame continues to move in a straight line at a constant velocity as it exits the magnetic field, and then it undergoes uniformly accelerated linear motion, with the equation $2g(h-l)=v^{2}-v_{0}^{2}$.
sub_question_3.step_9: By simultaneously solving step_7 and step_8 and substituting the data, the velocity of the wire frame upon landing is found to be $v=4\mathrm{m/s}$.

sub_question_4.step_10: From the conservation of energy, we know that $Q=2m g l$. Thus, $Q=0.4\mathrm{J}$.

image_captions: The image depicts a square conducting loop, labeled a,b,c,d, initially below a horizontal dashed line indicating the lower border of a uniform magnetic field directed into the page (indicated by "x" symbols). The loop has an initial upward velocity, labeled "v_0", perpendicular to the magnetic field. The loop's initial position is at a distance, "h", above a horizontal line that represents the ground.

step_1.physical_theorem: Faraday's Law of Electromagnetic Induction and Ohm's Law

result_quantity.name: Induced current magnitude when ab side enters magnetic field

step_2.physical_theorem: Newton's First Law (Equilibrium) and Ampere's force

result_quantity.name: Force balance equation when the frame moves at a constant velocity.
Magnetic induction intensity of the uniform magnetic field
Time taken for the wire frame to completely enter the magnetic field
Time taken for wire frame to reach the highest point after completely entering the field
Total time from entering magnetic field to reaching highest point

step_7.physical_theorem: Vertical projectile motion and Ampere's force

result_quantity.name: Force balance equation as the frame returns to the magnetic field boundary

step_8.physical_theorem: Kinematic equation for uniformly accelerated linear motion

result_quantity.name: Velocity of the wire frame upon landing
Joule heat generated by the wire frame during the entire process
